name: Ron McClure
abstract: Ron McClure (born November 22, 1941) is an American jazz bassist.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Steve Kuhn Quartet / Zane Massey Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve played with such a wide variety of bass players... from Ron Carter to Ron McClure
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: We played, Bertha, Ron McClure, and myself played a couple – two or three tunes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: So I went to guys I knew before. That was that quintet... McClure. I said, let’s get McClure. I know him from [?]. I know he’s steady and solid... That’s how that band finally got solidified by ’84 in that personnel.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum
about_quotes.evidence: eventually it was Ron McClure on bass. So these were all more experienced people than me. And to have that opportunity to play with them was really good
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: one time we had a band with myself and Mick Goodrick, also a great guitar player, and Ron McClure.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001
